(3) Portents of Water

If rivers recede from a town, or if those that never dry or
other places like lakes, dry up, the town would be desolate ere long.
If rivers flow carrying oil, blood or flesh, be turbulent or filthy or
flow upwards, they presage attack by a foreign enemy after six
months.
[$$ is a lake which has a perennial spring : ScT
HiM-owfd —remarks the commentator.]
^ uven
The emanation of flames, smoke, foam (boiling), sound of
weeping, shouting, singing or talking, from wells is declared as a
fore-runner of the mortality of the population.
ere n^oii
If water springs up from the earth that has not been dug, if
water acquires a different smell or taste, and if some unnatural
features are seen in tanks etc.
